subject: GRB 210615A: Fermi GBM Final Real-time Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely LONG GRB

At 23:33:53 UT on 15 Jun 2021, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM) triggered and located GRB 210615A (trigger 645492838.603514 / 210615982).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data, is RA = 342.9, Dec = 62.2 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 22h 51m, 62d 12'), with a statistical uncertainty of 7.5 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 19.0 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2021/bn210615982/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn210615982.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2021/bn210615982/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn210615982.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2021/bn210615982/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn210615982.gif
